metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun activities for your 3-year-old in Hackney! From barn dancing at Round Chapel to puppet workshops at Little Angel Theatre, there's something for everyone. And with Sunday's weather looking good for outdoor activities, get ready for a fantastic time!

activities.title: Cut A Shine St. Patrick's Day – Family Barn Dance
activities.description: A family barn dance with dances designed for the smallest kids, plus Irish stew and a kids' disco.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun, active, and suitable for all ages, offering a lively and engaging experience.

transport.fromHackney: Bus routes and overground to Hackney Downs or Hackney Central, followed by a short walk.
transport.tip: Check bus routes in advance as some may be affected by the St Patrick's Day Parade.

activities.title: Family Workshops - The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: Create spoon puppet versions of the characters in the story and miniature stage backdrops inspired by The Everywhere Bear.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on, creative, and linked to a well-loved story, perfect for engaging a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Islington, then a short walk, or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a bus.
transport.tip: Check which venue the workshop is at when booking, either the Theatre or the Studios.

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: A puppet show about a little old lady whose house feels too small, featuring a flappy, scratchy, greedy, noisy crowd of farmyard animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets, animals, and a simple, engaging story make this ideal for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for weekend showings, as availability is low.

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: A puppet show about The Everywhere Bear who has a wonderful time with the children in Class One, but one day he gets more than he bargained for.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging story and puppets, appealing to young children's sense of adventure.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Check the Little Angel Theatre website for showtimes and to book tickets.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Explore design and creativity at the Young V&A, with collections and displays designed to inspire young minds.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and a focus on design make this museum engaging for young children, and it's free.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Bethnal Green, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the website for any special events or workshops taking place.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: A city farm with donkeys, sheep, goats, and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals and outdoor space are always a hit with toddlers, and it's free!

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Shoreditch or Liverpool Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops on their website.

activities.title: Library Club
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxing and engaging activity for young children, promoting a love of reading.

transport.fromHackney: Local buses to Hackney Central, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: The Ga Legacy in Colour
activities.description: Vibrant exhibition dedicated to preserving and sharing the legacy of the Ga language from Accra, Ghana, in a contemporary form.
activities.whyItWorks: A colourful and engaging exhibition that introduces children to a different culture in a fun way.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Whitechapel, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Check the Half Moon website for opening times and any special events.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth with dinosaur fossils, meteorites, and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs and interactive exhibits make this a captivating and educational experience for young children, and it's free.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line directly to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Hands-on exhibits and interactive displays make learning about science fun for all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits are perfect for engaging young children's curiosity and making learning fun, and it's free.

transport.fromHackney: Piccadilly Line directly to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check for special events or demonstrations happening during your visit.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Enjoy the magnolias and cherry blossom, paired with musical compositions.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor space, beautiful blossoms, and gentle music create a sensory experience suitable for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: District Line to Kew Gardens station.
transport.tip: Head for Cherry Walk and Magnolia Walk, then finish near The Orangery café.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A riverside hub with art installations, family concerts, creative workshops, and open spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to roam, free activities, and a vibrant atmosphere make this a great day out.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Waterloo or Northern Line to Embankment, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for free events and activities.

activities.title: Queen's Walk
activities.description: A riverside stroll with views, buskers, and a merry-go-round.
activities.whyItWorks: Space to run around safely, entertainment, and iconic sights make this a fun and easy day out.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Waterloo or Northern Line to Embankment, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Stop for an ice cream at Oxo Tower.

activities.title: Jubilee Gardens Adventure Playground
activities.description: Adventure playground with sensory sound, vision, and touch experiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for letting off steam, with equipment designed to delight children of differing abilities.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Waterloo, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Pack a lunch to enjoy on the grass.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world art and artifacts from throughout history and around the globe.
activities.whyItWorks: Stimulating for a 3-year-old, with lots to see. Pick a specific area to focus on to avoid overwhelm.

transport.fromHackney: Central Line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check for family trails and activities on the website.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt at Ham House and Garden
activities.description: A seasonal trail set within one of south-west London's most intact 17th-century estates.
activities.whyItWorks: An outdoor adventure that will capture the imagination of a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Richmond, then a local bus or a walk.
transport.tip: Event runs daily from 10am to 5pm and is designed as a self-guided outdoor activity.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Follow the trail, matching the names of influential characters to the Lindt gold bunny statues and collecting a mini chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk and a chocolate treat at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early in the day – the grounds are calmer before lunch.

activities.title: London Zoo's Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore the Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Animals plus an Easter egg hunt equals a fun-filled day for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Regent's Park or Camden Town, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges and discover facts.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a popular children's show, engaging and educational.

transport.fromHackney: District Line to Kew Gardens station.
transport.tip: Allow 2-3 hours to do it properly.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books come to life on stage with Bear, Little Fish and the Turtles.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppets and a well-known story make this a great theatre experience for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance on the Little Angel Theatre website.

activities.title: The Koala Who Could
activities.description: An outback adventure about taking a chance, combining puppetry and songs.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppetry, songs, and a positive message about embracing change make this show engaging for 3-year-olds.

transport.fromHackney: Jubilee Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, as this is a popular show.

activities.title: Puppet Playcation - Superheroes
activities.description: A holiday workshop where children can create their own superhero puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative activity combined with the popular theme of superheroes

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Highbury & Islington, then a short bus ride or walk.
transport.tip: Check the Little Angel Theatre website for workshop times and booking.

activities.title: Family Fun at the Cutty Sark
activities.dates: Various dates in March and April
activities.description: Meet characters from the ship's past, follow a family trail, and join in musical storytelling sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational, with characters and activities to capture the imagination.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark station.
transport.tip: Check the Cutty Sark website for the schedule of activities.
